title: cistus × ingwersenii
intro: Cistus × ingwersenii, also known as the Hybrid Rockrose, is a hybrid plant species obtained from the cross-breeding of Cistus ladanifer and Cistus albidus. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins. The health benefits of Cistus × ingwersenii include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a valuable remedy for wound healing and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to support respiratory health and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis, coughs, and colds.
